content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and you have to perform the following operation exactly XXX times:


Choose two integers iii and jjj such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i \lt j \le N1≤i<j≤N.
Choose a non-negative integer ppp.
Change AiA_iAi​ to Ai⊕2pA_i \oplus 2^pAi​⊕2p, and change AjA_jAj​ to Aj⊕2pA_j \oplus 2^pAj​⊕2p, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR.
Find the lexicographically smallest sequence which can be obtained by performing this operation exactly XXX times.

A sequence B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ is said to be lexicographically smaller than a sequence C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​ if there is an index iii such that Bi<CiB_i \lt C_iBi​<Ci​ and for each valid j<ij \lt ij<i, Bj=CjB_j = C_jBj​=Cj​.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and X. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space-separated integers – the lexicographically smallest sequence obtainable after performing exactly X operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Three operations on indices (1,2) with p=1 toggle the least-significant set bit of the first two numbers, ending with the lexicographically smallest sequence.
Only one operation is allowed; using i=1, j=2, p=0 (2^0=1) yields the smallest possible first element.
Two operations on indices (1,2) with p=0 toggle the least-significant set bit of the first two numbers, ending with the lexicographically smallest sequence.

solution.approach: The key insight is to find the most significant set bit in each number and try to clear it by performing the XOR operation with another number that has the same bit set. This approach ensures that we get the lexicographically smallest sequence. We start from the first number and try to clear its most significant set bit. If we can't find another number with the same bit set, we move to the next number.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * log(max(Ai))) per test case, O(T * N * log(max(Ai))) total. The time complexity is due to the loop over each number and the loop to find the most significant set bit.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the array to store the input numbers.

code.Python3:
import sys

def msb_pos(x):
    return x.bit_length() - 1

input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not input_data:
    sys.exit()
it = iter(input_data)
T = int(next(it))
out_lines = []
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(next(it)); X = int(next(it))
    A = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
    ops = X
    for i in range(N-1):
        while A[i] and ops:
            b = msb_pos(A[i])
            mask = 1 << b
            j = i + 1
            while j < N and (A[j] & mask) == 0:
                j += 1
            if j == N: break
            A[i] ^= mask
            A[j] ^= mask
            ops -= 1
    if ops and N == 2 and (ops & 1):
        A[-2] ^= 1
        A[-1] ^= 1
    out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, A)))
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))
